name: Systems Languages

tasks.title: Author Go prompt
tasks.body: Write internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/go.md (40-80 lines). This file REPLACES coding/default.md for Go-classified tasks. Cover: idiomatic Go style (accept interfaces/return structs, error wrapping with %w, table-driven tests, short variable names in tight scopes), build/test tooling (go build, go test ./..., go vet, gofmt/goimports, golangci-lint), testing conventions (testify or stdlib, subtests, testdata/), and common pitfalls (nil pointer on interface, goroutine leaks, deferred close in loops, shadowed err). Use 'prefer X' phrasing. ZERO wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Author Rust prompt
tasks.body: Write internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/rust.md (40-80 lines). REPLACES coding/default.md for Rust-classified tasks. Cover: ownership/borrowing idioms, Result/Option patterns (prefer ? operator, avoid unwrap in library code), build/test tooling (cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy, cargo fmt, rustfmt.toml), testing conventions (unit tests in same file via #[cfg(test)] mod, integration tests in tests/), and common pitfalls (lifetime annotations, borrow checker fights from over-borrowing, String vs &str at API boundaries, async runtime selection). Use 'prefer X' phrasing. ZERO wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Author C++ prompt
tasks.body: Write internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/cpp.md (40-80 lines). REPLACES coding/default.md for C++-classified tasks. Cover: modern C++ idioms (RAII, smart pointers over raw, move semantics, constexpr where appropriate), build/test tooling (CMake, Make, clang-tidy, clang-format, cppcheck), testing conventions (Google Test, Catch2, CTest integration), and common pitfalls (undefined behavior, dangling references, implicit conversions, header-only vs compiled libraries, include-what-you-use). Use 'prefer X' phrasing. ZERO wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Author C prompt
tasks.body: Write internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/c.md (40-80 lines). REPLACES coding/default.md for C-classified tasks. Cover: idiomatic C style (explicit memory management, consistent error return patterns, defensive initialization), build/test tooling (Make/CMake, gcc/clang, valgrind, AddressSanitizer, clang-format), testing conventions (Unity, CMocka, or project-established framework), and common pitfalls (buffer overflows, use-after-free, null dereference, integer overflow, missing include guards, POSIX portability). Use 'prefer X' phrasing. ZERO wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Systems Languages is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify go.md, rust.md, cpp.md, and c.md each follow the section structure from coding/default.md (Design or Style, Build and Test, Testing, pitfalls) and contain language-specific tool invocations, not generic advice
